title: schoenus multiglumis
intro: Schoenus multiglumis, also known as the marsh club-rush, is a type of sedge that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. It is native to Australia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Schoenus multiglumis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antiviral activity, making it useful for treating various health conditions such as skin infections and respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, Schoenus multiglumis can be used as a tincture, infused in tea, or applied topically in the form of a poultice or salve, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
